patterns.description: User provides a URL/niche and requests full PPC analysis: competitors + keywords + audience + campaigns + media plan. This is the primary use case of the PPC Master Tool.
patterns.routing_note: meta-orchestrator should always route this to ppc-market-analyst agent

patterns.description: User requests CPC benchmarks to calibrate the PPC Master Tool budget calculation system. Default CPC values (21-55 RUB) are systematically underestimated for Moscow competitive niches. Building materials/roofing in Moscow: real search CPC is 35-120 RUB depending on query intent and season.
patterns.routing_note: When user asks to verify/calibrate CPC or budget defaults, treat as high-priority calibration task. Always cross-reference with niche + region + season.

patterns.description: User requests deep niche market research for a specific game genre (MMORPG) to build a knowledge base for PPC campaigns. Pattern: niche + region + structured output (titles, audience, targeting, keywords, negatives). Result saved to knowledge-base as JSON for reuse in automated campaign generation.
patterns.routing_note: When user requests niche-level research (not per-site), output should be saved to knowledge-base/assets/ as a structured JSON file for future automated campaign use. Always check if the niche file already exists before running fresh research.

patterns.description: In RU market analysis, many games and services have geo-restrictions or alternative access methods. Always flag geo-blocked titles and provide alternative access notes in the analysis. Affects: WoW (no direct payment), Throne and Liberty (Steam blocked), some Steam titles.
patterns.routing_note: For any gaming niche in RU market: add a geo-restriction check step. Document workarounds (VPN, third-party resellers, local publishers) as these directly affect ad copy and landing page strategy.

patterns.description: User requests a detailed media plan for a specific F2P game title on Yandex Direct with large budget (1M+ RUB/month). Pattern involves: multi-campaign structure (branded search + genre search + RSA + retargeting + master + display), phased bidding strategy (manual -> auto CPA), flighting with peak-hour adjustments (19:00-01:00 gaming audience), and weekly KPI monitoring thresholds.
patterns.routing_note: For game title media plans: always check mmo-population.com for current player count, verify geo-restrictions, use 5-cluster semantic structure (branded + genre_f2p + competitive + guides + download). Retargeting warmup takes 2-4 weeks. Blended CPR benchmark for F2P MMORPG: 400-600 RUB.

patterns.description: User requests PPC strategy for a game that is not yet released (pre-launch / CBT stage). Target action is registration (CPR), not install (CPI). Key challenges: (1) brand name confusion with non-gaming entities (Bellatores vs Bellator MMA), (2) no existing player base for lookalike/retargeting, (3) landing page must be registration-focused. Strategy: awareness + registration collection phase before ЗБТ, then install/play phase at launch.
patterns.routing_note: For pre-launch games in RU market: CPR target 300-600 RUB; primary platform Yandex Direct (search + RSA); VK Ads for community-based targeting (competitor game groups); Telegram Ads for gaming media channels. Always add non-gaming homophones to negative keywords list. Phased budget: Awareness phase 30-40% of launch budget.
